title: build: allow to build without cgo on RISC-V
body:
a few fixes to allow building on platforms where cgo is not available.

The generated podman is not really usable, as it misses the support for shm locking.  Should we restore back file locking in this case?

Rootless support is also missing.

These two updates are also required (I'll revendor once they are merged):

https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/375
https://github.com/containers/psgo/pull/53

@carlosedp I could build  podman for RISC-V using these commands:

```
export GO=/path/to/riscv-go/bin/go
export BUILDTAGS_CROSS="containers_image_openpgp containers_image_ostree_stub exclude_graphdriver_btrfs exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper exclude_disk_quota"
make bin/podman.cross.linux.riscv64
```

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Locking is an interface, so it should be possible to redo SHM locking
within the new system. Would need a config knob to select which locking
implementation to use.

Testing it right now. I'll report back in a few.

@giuseppe I've successfully built podman on the SiFive Unleashed board (real hardware)! I also built `crun` and `conmon` to support it.

```
➜ go build -tags "containers_image_openpgp containers_image_ostree_stub exclude_graphdriver_btrfs exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper exclude_disk_quota" ./cmd/podman

➜ ./podman --version
podman version 1.4.4-dev
WARN[0000] unable to find /home/carlosedp/.config/containers/registries.conf. some podman (image shortnames) commands may be limited

➜ sudo cp ../conmon/bin/conmon /usr/local/bin
 
➜ ./podman --runtime=/usr/local/bin/crun
Error: could not get runtime: this function is not supported on this os
WARN[0000] unable to find /home/carlosedp/.config/containers/registries.conf. some podman (image shortnames) commands may be limited
```

Checking what's required to run the full podman stack.
@carlosedp rootless won't work without cgo :/ you'll need to use root
also you need to set `lock_type ="file"` in `/etc/containers/libpod.conf`

The images were build correctly. I added the directive to the libpod.conf and changed the default runtime to crun. I followed the pre-reqs install from XXXX but got a prob running the container:

Ah nice, I downloaded it as 99-loopback as the install instructions.

I'm getting some iptables errors.. maybe on the Risc-V package:

```
➜ sudo podman run -d -p 8080:8080 test-echo
Error: error adding firewall rules for container 9f737d4ac4889fc1ee2a270df1f7ef9f8762940dc26b679c585b29cbe2b47eb2: failed to add the address 10.88.0.4/32 to trusted zone: COMMAND_FAILED: '/usr/sbin/iptables-restore -w -n' failed: iptables-restore v1.8.0 (legacy): Couldn't load target `PRE_trusted':No such file or directory

Error occurred at line: 2
Try `iptables-restore -h' or 'iptables-restore --help' for more information.
```
could you try first if it works in the host network namespace? `--net host`

@vrothberg tests are failing because of the change I've added to psgo:

```
Error: open /proc/self/auxv: no such file or directory
```

which is strange, it works fine here on Fedora 30.  Are we running the tests without `/proc` being mounted?

> @vrothberg tests are failing because of the change I've added to psgo:
> 
> ```
> Error: open /proc/self/auxv: no such file or directory
> ```
> 
> which is strange, it works fine here on Fedora 30. Are we running the tests without `/proc` being mounted?

`/proc` must be present since the rest of `psgo` is already parsing it. Is `auxv` a configurable?

> `/proc` must be present since the rest of `psgo` is already parsing it. Is `auxv` a configurable?

AFAICS it should be always present
@vrothberg if you are fine I can restore the previous C code and use the `/proc/self/auxv` only when cgo is not present, what do you think?

@carlosedp that is great news!  The network is something fishy with CNI
once https://github.com/containers/psgo/pull/54 is merged, I will revendor and rebase

> @vrothberg if you are fine I can restore the previous C code and use the `/proc/self/auxv` only when cgo is not present, what do you think?

Yes, I'm okay with that but before merging it, I'd like to check why `auxv` wasn't present.
revendored, tests seem happy now
@giuseppe Are you still working on this, with @vrothberg last comment on the locking?

FWIW, I am currently working a new locking package in c/storage which is based on flock(2). The advantage of flock(2) is that it allows for tryLock semantics. Giuseppe found that those are incompatible with the fcntl-based ones, so we can't migrate the storage-locks. But new users could jump on that.

We can probably migrate after the fact with minimal difficulty... Make
`system renumber` wipe the file locks as well and run it in rpm postinstall.

This needs manpage updates for libpod.conf for the new locking field. Also,
ideally, we should leave it commented out in the config file, and have a
comment saying to only use this if you know what you're doing, and run a
`system renumber` after switching (won't do anything now, but good practice
- if we ever need migration code we can throw it in there).

Also, would be nice to have a default of `shm` in the default config.
